title: Quiz: Quantum Optimization Landscapes
description: Test understanding of optimization landscape concepts

questions.question: What characterizes a saddle point in the optimization landscape?

options.text: Hessian has both positive and negative eigenvalues

questions.explanation: A saddle point has zero gradient but mixed curvature - the Hessian has both positive and negative eigenvalues.

questions.question: At a local minimum, the Hessian matrix is:
questions.explanation: At a local minimum, all second derivatives are positive (bowl-shaped), making the Hessian positive definite.

questions.question: VQE optimization landscapes are always convex.
questions.explanation: VQE landscapes are generally non-convex with multiple local minima, making optimization challenging.

questions.question: The gradient at a critical point is:
questions.explanation: By definition, critical points (minima, maxima, saddle points) have zero gradient.

questions.question: Multiple random restarts help with optimization because:

options.text: They explore different basins to find better minima

questions.explanation: Different initializations can converge to different local minima, allowing exploration of multiple basins.
